Jamie Dimon says insecurity, not ego, is what destroys the careers of top CEOs
description: The JPMorgan CEO says leaders get into trouble when their insecurity discourages employees from delivering bad news.
fullText:
Jamie Dimon, one of the banking industry's longest-serving CEOs, has thoughts on why some CEOs rise to the occasion while others flounder—and it has little to do with how much they know about business.

Dimon, who has served as CEO of JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. bank by market cap, for 20 years, said on the Master Investor Podcast in an interview published on Monday that, more than anything, insecurity is one of the biggest threats to a CEO's career and their company's success.

As Dimon puts it, when executives climb the corporate ladder, they often go from being an expert overseeing one area of the business they know well to a generalist responsible for other areas of the company of which they're unfamiliar.

"The bigger job you get, the less you know—literally—about the job," he told the podcast's host Wildfred Frost.

Some CEOs handle that transition well, he said. They seek out people with expertise they lack and don't get embarrassed when they don't know something.

But for others, this change is a recipe for insecurity.

"It becomes PowerPoints, you know, make them look good, don't tell them the bad news. And that is what causes the problem," he said.

Dimon said this type of insecurity, more than arrogance, speaks to a deeper weakness in leadership that causes other issues. A CEO needs to be able to absorb criticism and turn it into a plan for improvement, especially when it comes to customer complaints, he said.

"You're not going to hurt my feelings by telling me we have a crappy product," he said. "If you're right, I'm gonna thank you for letting me know."

Dimon's record gives his leadership advice added weight. During his tenure, he has turned JPMorgan into a $917 billion banking giant. His "fortress balance sheet" strategy, which emphasizes high liquidity and conservative risk management, was key to helping the bank survive the 2008 recession. Dimon has also helped grow the bank over the past two decades by acquiring competitors in turmoil, such as Bear Stearns in 2008 and, more recently, First Republic Bank in 2023.

His latest comments on insecurity are consistent with his previous warnings that "bureaucracy, complacency, and arrogance," can take down otherwise successful companies.

In April, Dimon said the best way to tackle these problems is to "get rid of the jerks," or bureaucratic managers who care more about the process than the outcome. Holding back information, as might happen with an insecure CEO, breeds "rope-a-dope politics," which he has also said creates unnecessary debate and hinders results.

title: 6 dementia symptoms that can show up years before a diagnosis
description: Impaired memory and thinking are the most common symptoms associated with a dementia diagnosis. But noncognitive symptoms can appear years or even decades earlier. Alzheimer's disease, which accounts ...
fullText:
Impaired memory and thinking are the most common symptoms associated with a dementia diagnosis. But noncognitive symptoms can appear years or even decades earlier.

Alzheimer's disease, which accounts for 60 to 80 percent of dementia cases, can progress through three main stages. The first stage can start 15 to 20 years before any obvious changes to cognition. During this time, the brain starts to shrink, and the misfolded proteins that are a hallmark of Alzheimer's begin to build up - sticky amyloid beta plaques in the brain and tau tangles in neurons.

This is followed by mild cognitive impairment, where people begin having noticeable problems with memory and cognition but otherwise have good overall function. Then comes the "dementia" stage, when multiple cognitive domains are impaired, leading to an inability to function independently in day-to-day life.

Scientists think the biological changes to the brain during the first and second stages may cause emotional, sensory and physical symptoms even years before a potential diagnosis.

"You can have many of these other precursors but not have any cognitive symptoms," said Timothy Chang, an associate professor of neurology at UCLA.

Of course, experts caution that having early symptoms linked to an increased risk of Alzheimer's does not mean you'll necessarily develop dementia, and about 45 percent of dementia cases are preventable with lifestyle changes.

Here are six early symptoms that often appear in people who are later diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

Depression is a known risk factor for dementia. A 2023 study of more than 1.4 million Danish adults found that the risk of dementia was 2.4 times higher in those diagnosed with depression.

But depression and anxiety that develop later in life - around age 55 or 60 - can also be warning signs that something is amiss.

"The idea is that some of the brain network changes that are happening early in dementia affect your mental health as well," so changes to mental health are "a signal for early potential changes in brain function," Chang said.

Having these mood symptoms may also accelerate the progression of dementia, researchers have found.

Neuropsychiatric symptoms such as irritability and agitation are common in Alzheimer's disease and can be found before cognitive decline occurs.

One 2017 study of about 1,400 people found that about 31 percent of older cognitively healthy adults had mood symptoms, compared with about 47 percent among those with increased dementia risk and about 54 percent in people who had mild cognitive impairment.

About 50 to 60 percent of people with biomarkers of Alzheimer's pathology have at least one neuropsychiatric symptom, said Adam Turnbull, an instructor in psychiatry and behavioral sciences at Stanford University who wrote a recent paper about disruptions to emotion regulation in preclinical Alzheimer's disease.

"If you just feel like you're mad and grumpy all the time, and you're snapping at people and your relationships with people seem to be highlighted by kind of consistent fighting and kind of negative vibes, then go seek mental health support" from a geriatric psychiatrist, Turnbull said.

Because sleeping well can be protective, poor sleep can serve as "an indicator of something going wrong," Turnbull said.

Sleep apnea and other sleep disorders, including excessive daytime sleepiness, restless leg syndrome and circadian rhythm sleep disorders, are associated with increased dementia risk, according to a 2025 meta-analysis of 39 studies.

Insomnia or reduced nighttime rest can also precede dementia. One study of more than 4,400 cognitively healthy older adults found that having less nighttime sleep or more daytime sleep was linked to an increased accumulation of amyloid beta - a biological hallmark of Alzheimer's disease - in the brain.

On the other hand, each additional hour of nighttime sleep was linked to reduced amyloid beta levels. Research suggests that sleep may help flush out waste, including amyloid beta, from the brain.

Of course, you shouldn't worry about an afternoon nap. But sleeping a lot during the day, and then not sleeping well at night, may be a cause for concern, Turnbull said.

Our ability to smell is linked to brain health, and losing this sense is often one of the first signs of neurodegenerative disorders, including Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease. About 85 percent of people with early-stage Alzheimer's have problems smelling.

The nose has a unique connection with the brain. Compared with other senses, such as hearing and sight, information for smell is sent more directly to the cortex where it then connects to areas important for cognition, memory and mood, including the hippocampus and the amygdala.

Intriguingly, the smell - or olfactory - nerve is able to regenerate, and people can improve their smell with training. There is even preliminary evidence that smell training can improve cognition, even for people with a normal ability to smell.

For years, changes such as walking more slowly, taking shorter steps or having a wider stride were considered a normal part of aging. But research now suggests that these changes can precede and predict cognitive impairment and other neurological problems.

A 2024 study of more than 1,300 healthy adults older than 65 found that walking ability - including walking speed and the ability to turn 360 degrees - diminished up to a decade before cognitive decline.

Grip strength is closely linked with healthy aging and may be a better indicator of life expectancy than blood pressure.

Weaker grip strength is also associated with worse cognitive health. One recent study analyzing over 190,000 adults found every 11-pound decrease in hand grip strength was associated with a 20 percent increase in dementia in men and a 12 percent increase in women. Weaker handgrip strength was linked to lower problem-solving scores and worse performance on a memory task, as well.

Grip strength is one of the ways doctors assess physical frailty, along with how you walk. In medical terms, physical frailty means your body is more vulnerable to illness and injury. It's both a risk factor for dementia and a potential early warning sign that can appear about a decade before cognitive symptoms begin.

"If you're an older adult noticing these symptoms, I wouldn't panic and assume it's dementia," Turnbull said. "There's a good chance it's not anything to do with anything neurological."

But if you're concerned about your memory or thinking, especially alongside any of these symptoms, it's important to talk to your doctor. Recent advances in diagnostics, including for PET scans, lumbar puncture and a new blood test, can detect Alzheimer's biomarkers earlier, which in turn could speed up treatments.

"I think it's good to have an evaluation and have a physician kind of give feedback on like, oh, this is concerning or it's not," Chang said.

There's a genetic component to dementia risk, but there are also many ways we can reduce our risk.

Last year, the U.S. POINTER clinical trial found that a mix of exercise, a healthy diet, social engagement and brain games could improve cognitive capabilities in older adults at risk of cognitive decline or dementia.

Other studies have found that eliminating alcohol, getting regular vaccines and protecting our hearing could help preserve cognition.

Video: Columnist Richard Sima got his sense of smell tested at Johns Hopkins University. Here's what he learned about how smell impacts his brain health, and how you can train yours.(c) 2026 , The Washington Post

title: UK humanitarian worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital
description: The health worker was evacuated to the UK from the DR Congo after being potentially exposed to the virus.
fullText:
A humanitarian worker in the Democratic Republic of Congo has been medically evacuated to the UK after a potential healthcare‑related exposure to Ebola.

The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said the individual, who is a UK resident and has not been named, has no symptoms, and the move is purely precautionary.

The individual remains well but is being assessed and monitored at a London hospital, the statement from UKHSA said.

The hospital has not been named but the Royal Free in north London has a high level isolation unit and is the primary national centre for high consequence infectious diseases.

The incubation period of Ebola ranges from two to 21 days and the humanitarian worker will be required to isolate throughout.

The individual had been working with a humanitarian organisation supporting the Ebola outbreak response.

Ebola is a highly dangerous pathogen. Unlike flu or Covid, it is not airborne and spreads through direct contact with bodily fluids.

Symptoms come on suddenly and start like the flu or malaria, with fever, headache and tiredness. As the disease progresses, vomiting and diarrhoea develop and it can lead to organ failure. Some, but not all, patients develop internal and external bleeding.

Richard Pebody, director of epidemic and emerging infections at UKHSA, said: "The risk to the general public remains low.

"This individual has been transferred out of an abundance of caution, and we're pleased they remain well."

There are currently no confirmed cases of Ebola in the UK, the agency said.

There were three confirmed Ebola cases in the UK between 2014 and 2015 - all involving healthcare workers returning from west Africa. All recovered and there was no onward transmission.

According to the World Health Organization, as of 19 July the DR Congo government has recorded 2,423 confirmed cases of Ebola, including 967 deaths, since the outbreak was declared in mid-May. The WHO said 469 had recovered.

Uganda has recorded another 20 confirmed cases and two deaths.

The latest outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo species of Ebola, for which there is no vaccine, although several are in development.

It has been described as the third-worst outbreak on record by WHO head Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus.

Get our flagship newsletter with all the headlines you need to start the day. 

What is Ebola and why is stopping the latest outbreak so difficult?

'Speed, money and compassion' - lessons from an Ebola survivor and other experts

Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak

title: OpenAI says its AI technology acted on its own in an 'unprecedented' hack of another company
description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Tuesday that its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an “unprecedented cyber incident.”  “We had a significant...
fullText:
ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Tuesday that its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

"We had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a statement posted on social media.

AI startup Hugging Face said last week that it had detected an intrusion into its data processing systems that it suspected was caused by an AI agent autonomously acting on its own.

"We suspected last week's cyberattack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent," Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clément Delangue said in a statement. "Turns out it did!"

The disclosure comes amid heightened concerns about the cybersecurity capabilities of powerful models that led President Donald Trump in June to sign an executive order creating a framework for the federal government to vet the national security risks of the most advanced AI systems for up to a month before their public release.

"AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities."

Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part. It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!"

Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."

OpenAI said the intrusion was caused by a combination of its AI models, including its newly released GPT‑5.6 Sol and an "even more capable" model that is still being tested internally.

OpenAI said its AI used stolen credentials and discovered a previously unknown vulnerability to access Hugging Face servers.

It went to "extreme lengths to achieve a rather narrow testing goal" and "found ways to gain access to secret information that it could use to cheat the evaluation," the company said.

title: Murdered ex-UK minister Widdecombe was struck on head with hammer 21 times, court told
description: LONDON, July 21 (Reuters) - Former British government minister Ann Widdecombe was killed by a man who struck her on the head 21 times with a hammer while ‌she was eating lunch at home earlier this mon...
fullText:
LONDON, July 21 (Reuters) - Former British government minister Ann Widdecombe was killed by a man who struck her on the head 21 times with a hammer while ‌she was eating lunch at home earlier this month, prosecutors told a London court on Tuesday.

Widdecombe, ‌78, a prominent figure in Nigel Farage's populist Reform UK party, was found dead at her home in rural southwest ​England on July 9 after being attacked the day before.

The killing shocked British politics, raising further concerns about politicians' security, with two serving British members of parliament having been killed since 2016.

Joshua Kerry, a white British national from Rotherham in northern England, more than 250 miles (402 km) from Widdecombe's home, appeared in ‌court on Tuesday charged with her ⁠murder.

The bearded 28-year-old, who appeared in the dock at London's Westminster Magistrates' Court wearing a grey tracksuit, was not asked to enter a plea.

At a later ⁠hearing at the Old Bailey court, Kerry was remanded in custody until his next appearance in October.

Police have said they believe Widdecombe was targeted, although the motive remains under investigation. Prosecutors said an investigation into possible ​terrorism ​offences was ongoing.

CAMERA IN HOME CAPTURED INCIDENT

Prosecutor Kashif Malik ​earlier said a camera inside Widdecombe's home ‌had captured the attack.

Widdecombe had been due to be interviewed live on television on July 8 from her home but did not appear, Malik explained. The following day, her personal assistant asked her gardener to check on Widdecombe, and he found her on the kitchen floor.

Malik told the court that the recovered footage showed Kerry entering Widdecombe's home while she was eating lunch and asking: "Don't suppose you ‌have bank cards and ID?"

Kerry, who was wearing black ​gloves and holding a hammer to his side so it ​was hidden from Widdecombe's view, then struck ​her 21 times, Malik said.

He added that Kerry had tipped Widdecombe out of ‌her chair onto the floor before taking ​her wallet and leaving, having ​been in the property for approximately two minutes.

Widdecombe was known for her socially conservative views, first as a junior minister in Conservative Prime Minister John Major's 1992-1997 government and later ​as an immigration and justice ‌spokesperson for Reform UK.

After stepping down from parliament in 2010, she appeared in a number ​of reality TV shows, including "Strictly Come Dancing".

title: House narrowly backs allowing troops, Pentagon workers to carry personal guns on military bases
description: The House voted 215-214 to presume approval when qualified service members and civilian employees request to carry personal firearms on bases.
fullText:
The House voted 215-214 Tuesday night to make approval the default when qualified service members and Pentagon civilian employees ask commanders for permission to carry personal firearms on military installations.

Three Democrats joined most Republicans to approve the measure, while three Republicans and independent Rep. Kevin Kiley of California opposed it.

The amendment from Rep. Jeff Crank, R-Colo., would not automatically authorize personnel to carry firearms on military property. It would establish a presumption that installation commanders approve requests from qualified service members and civilian employees.

The vote came less than a year after an Army sergeant allegedly used a personal handgun to shoot five fellow soldiers at Fort Stewart, Georgia, in August 2025. Other soldiers tackled and disarmed him, and all five victims survived. Personal firearms were prohibited on post at the time, military officials said.

Major Appeals Court Declares New Jersey Ar-15 Ban Unconstitutional In Landmark Second Amendment Ruling

The National Defense Authorization Act has become law every year for more than six decades and is traditionally one of Congress' most bipartisan major bills, but the firearms and transgender-policy provisions adopted Tuesday threatened to erode Democratic support, potentially forcing Republican leaders to rely on their narrow majority for final passage and creating new sticking points in eventual negotiations with the Senate.

Lawmakers approved an amendment from Rep. Nancy Mace, R-S.C., prohibiting TRICARE from covering transition-related hormones, puberty blockers and surgeries for service members and military dependents.

The provision passed 219-208, with five Democrats voting yes and one Republican voting no. It would broaden existing Trump administration restrictions and write the prohibition into federal law.

Senate Republican Pushes Merit-based Military Promotions, Targets Biden-era Dei Policies

The House also voted 221-203 to approve a second Mace amendment barring transgender girls from participating in girls' sports at Department of Defense Education Activity schools serving military families.

But lawmakers rejected a separate amendment from Rep. Lauren Boebert, R-Colo., that would have codified President Donald Trump's 2025 executive order barring transgender troops from military service and requiring personnel to serve according to their biological sex.

The amendment failed 217-212, with four Republicans joining all voting Democrats in opposition.

Lawmakers also rejected an effort from Rep. Eli Crane, R-Ariz., to largely eliminate funding for the Pentagon's Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative. The amendment would have permitted funding only for security at the U.S. Embassy in Kyiv.

Seventy-six Republicans supported the proposal, highlighting continued opposition within the GOP conference to military assistance for Ukraine.

The House rejected another proposal that would have barred foreign nationals from attending U.S. military academies in a 360-61 vote. The amendment would have ended longstanding programs that train officers from allied and partner nations at institutions including West Point, the Naval Academy and the Air Force Academy.

Among other amendments, lawmakers voted to eliminate a proposed pilot program that would have used voice-based risk-assessment technology to identify service members for drug testing.

The House also narrowly approved language intended to ensure the continued operation of California's Santa Ynez Pipeline System, which transports crude oil to refineries supplying military installations. That measure passed 215-214.

The votes came as lawmakers began working through amendments to the fiscal year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act. The House made 320 amendments eligible for consideration, eight more than were included in the procedural package that collapsed last month amid a Republican revolt over the SAVE America Act.

Amendment debate is expected to resume Wednesday, with a vote on final passage after lawmakers finish considering the remaining proposals.

The underlying bill would authorize roughly $1.15 trillion for national defense, about $252 billion more than Congress appropriated for defense programs in fiscal year 2026. It would provide pay raises ranging from 5% to 7% for service members, expand weapons and munitions production and accelerate investment in missile defense, autonomous systems, hypersonics and other emerging technologies.

The NDAA sets defense policy and authorizes spending levels, but it does not provide the money itself. Congress must separately pass defense appropriations legislation to fund the programs and priorities included in the bill.

The legislation would also formally change the Department of Defense's name to the Department of War, deeming references to the defense secretary and department in federal law to mean the secretary and Department of War.

Consideration of the legislation had been delayed for three weeks by a separate Republican fight over the SAVE America Act, Trump's election legislation requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote and identification to cast a ballot in federal elections.

The House approved the SAVE America Act in February by a 218-213 vote, but the measure stalled in the Senate, where Republicans lacked the 60 votes needed to overcome a filibuster.

Republican leaders initially sought to revive the election legislation by directing the House clerk to merge it with the NDAA after the defense bill passed but before the combined package was sent to the Senate. That approach would transmit the two measures as a single bill without making the SAVE America Act part of the NDAA text debated on the floor.

But the procedural rule authorizing that plan failed 198-224 on June 30, when 13 Republican holdouts joined Democrats in opposition. House Majority Leader Steve Scalise, R-La., also voted against the rule for procedural reasons.

Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla., argued that attaching the election measure through the procedural maneuver would make it easier for the Senate to remove and demanded that lawmakers add it directly to the NDAA.

Luna later accepted the original approach after Speaker Mike Johnson committed to attaching the SAVE America Act to appropriations and other must-pass legislation.

The House approved a replacement rule Tuesday that again directed the clerk to merge the election legislation with the NDAA before sending it to the Senate while expanding the number of defense amendments eligible for consideration from 312 to 320.

The SAVE America Act was still expected to face opposition in the Senate and could be removed when lawmakers reconcile the House and Senate versions of the defense bill.

The Senate's NDAA has faced problems of its own. Senate Democrats blocked consideration of their chamber's defense bill last week in protest of the Trump administration's war with Iran, leaving both chambers with work to do before they can negotiate a final package.

The debate comes as the Pentagon seeks additional money to cover the Iran war and replenish weapons stockpiles. The White House has requested an $87.6 billion supplemental package, including $67.1 billion for defense needs.

House Republicans are also considering a new reconciliation package that could provide $60 billion through the Armed Services Committee and $13 billion for intelligence programs. Reconciliation would allow the package to bypass a Senate filibuster and pass without Democratic support, but the proposal still faces resistance from GOP fiscal hawks and an uncertain path through Congress.

Original article

title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
description: A French professional model recruiter who for over a decade arranged for the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein to meet multiple models, was found dead in his home near Paris on Monday.
fullText:
A French professional model recruiter who for over a decade arranged for the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein to meet multiple models, was found dead in his home near Paris on Monday.

Daniel Siad's body was found in his house in Colombes, a suburb of the French capital, Marie-Céline Lawrysz, deputy public prosecutor at the Nanterre Judicial Court, told CNN in a Wednesday statement.

An autopsy will be performed on the body to determine the cause of death.

CNN'S Katie Polglase recently interviewed Siad, who said he had no reason to believe two women he had recommended to Epstein – and who had told CNN that Epstein had abused them – had been harmed by him.

Even after Epstein's 2008 conviction on sex crimes, modeling-industry insiders like Siad continued seeking business ventures with him, invited him to fashion events, and allowed him to associate with their companies.

Siad said Epstein had assured him he had paid for his crimes and that "this would never happen with anybody" that Siad referred to him.

"I trusted him and believed this guy was a professional person," he told CNN. "I never heard anything from anyone I introduced to him who came back to me that they had a bad situation. I believed this guy is a professional person."

Siad said he had believed Epstein was a casting director for Victoria's Secret and the modeling agency MC2. CNN found no evidence to support that claim.

Files released by the Department of Justice show Epstein paid tens of thousands of dollars to Siad.

In one message, Siad wrote: "Cute French girl in Marrakesh… She said that she will (be) happy to meet you."

Siad separately said that Epstein had asked him for help finding an assistant.

In a 2018 email, Siad wrote that he was looking for a "good looking young assistant" for Epstein.

In another message, Siad sent a photo to Epstein of a woman, describing her as "very polite" and someone who has "potential" as a "model" or "assistant."

Siad repeatedly sent Epstein photos of young women he encountered during his travels, some of them in provocative poses, according to the DOJ files.

Siad's name appears more than 2,000 times in the DOJ's Epstein files, CNN affiliate BFMTV reported.

Epstein's ties to the modeling industry are part of ongoing investigations, which include a criminal probe launched this year in Paris that is reviewing information related to Siad, according to the city's chief prosecutor.

Siad was also facing accusations of abuse. A model also accused him of rape when she was working in France in 1990. Siad denied the allegation.

CNN's Katie Polglase, Barbara Arvanitidis, Elina Baudier Kim, Curt Devine and Isabelle Chapman contributed reporting

title: EXCLUSIVE: Nick Shirley fires back at Minnesota critics: 'Numbers don't lie, and convictions don't lie'
description: YouTube independent journalist Nick Shirley has dominated the national conversation with his unorthodox investigations into daycare fraud, the border crisis and the fentanyl epidemic.
fullText:
Nick Shirley never planned to become one of the nation's best known independent journalists, making videos that would dominate the national conversation and be retweeted by vice presidents and CEOs.

Yet, in the wake of his Minnesota fraud investigation, Shirley has exploded onto the national scene as a one-man journalism machine.

Recently, he sat down with Fox News Digital at Freedom Fest in Las Vegas to discuss his unconventional rise, his reporting philosophy and the stories the mainstream media is still ignoring.

Nick Shirley Explains Fraud 'Entrepreneurship' He Says Is Running Rampant In Ca

"Well, when I was younger, I'd make vlog pranks, and then I started to get a little bit older and wanted to see what was happening in the world. And it turned into journalism," Shirley said about his start in reporting. "I didn't know it was journalism at the time. I thought I was just making YouTube videos on more interesting topics.

"And now I'm one of the biggest and most well-known journalists in America — in the world, actually."

Shirley conceded his particular style is hard to define.

"I think that's a good mix, like an … independent YouTube journalist, like it's journalism with a bit of documentary style as well. So, it's kind of different than what anyone has ever done before as far as this new realm of YouTube journalism," he said.

Shirley said he realized he had an explosive story as the Minnesota fraud video "Feeding Our Future" scandal cracked nine-digit views on X.

Youtuber To Testify Before Congress On Minnesota's Massive $9B Fraud Network Investigation

"When I saw it hit 100 million views, I had never seen a video get to 100 million views on X before. And then it became shared by some of the biggest voices in the world as well," said Shirley, who boasts 1.7 million followers on the platform and nearly 2 million subscribers on YouTube.

Among them were Vice President JD Vance, Elon Musk and FBI Director Kash Patel.

Shirley said he believes in zeroing his investigations on primary sources when he is devoting time and resources to new stories.

"I take all the information that's public from the government, and I go straight to the source," he said.

Despite the massive success Shirley has seen, he still comes across stories that do not get the exposure he feels is warranted.

"On a national level, I think the fentanyl crisis in the United States is really bad," Shirley said. "I've done a few videos on that, but people seem to not want to stop the fentanyl crisis from happening."

Owner Of Daycare In Viral Nick Shirley Video Charged In $4.6M Fraud Scheme, Prosecutors Say

Unsurprisingly, from time to time, Shirley said his journalistic work takes him to the limits of his comfort zone.

"In the favelas of Brazil, I thought I was going to die. That was a little scary," he said. "In Kenya and Philadelphia as well, that was pretty dangerous."

Shirley cited his Spanish language skills as a motivating factor in his coverage of the Biden administration border crisis, which he witnessed firsthand.

"I had heard about what was happening. I was living in Chile at the time. And I started to see what was happening with Venezuelan migrants coming into Chile," Shirley said. "When I came back to the United States, I started looking into what was going on at the southern border.

"And since I could speak Spanish, I started to reveal what was actually happening. And people didn't know that children were being smuggled. Seventeen-year-old girls were coming over all by themselves. Twelve-year-old girls were all coming over all by themselves. And I kind of started to showcase what was happening from the border all the way to the shelters in New York City."

Shirley did a two-year mission with the LDS Church, and credits his faith as a motivation for his work.

"Well, I think our faith invites us to look for the truth and expose evil," he said. "Like, the scriptures, the Bible, everything, it's a bunch of strong men exposing what's happening as well, and I think that's kind of lost."

Shirley said the recent guilty plea of Fahima Egeh Mahamud, the woman tied to Minnesota's massive "Feeding Our Future" scandal and daycare fraud scheme, as evidence of the veracity of his work, as the mainstream media and Democratic establishment have worked hard to discredit his investigations.

"Well, numbers don't lie, and convictions don't lie either," he said. "The largest daycare in Minnesota, government-funded, just pleaded guilty today."

Original article

title: Meet the teenager tasked with filling Alexia Putellas’ boots at Barcelona
description: “They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. I mean … we play very well,” Clara Serrajordi said with a laugh after reaching the Champions League final at Camp Nou last May, beating Bayern Munich in last season’s semi-finals. The 18-year-old midfielder’s confusion with “they” and “we” makes sense. Until last season, she was on the outside looking in. Serrajordi was used to watching the first team from her home at La Masia, the famed Barcelona youth academy known fo
fullText:
“They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. I mean … we play very well,” Clara Serrajordi said with a laugh after reaching the Champions League final at Camp Nou last May, beating Bayern Munich in last season’s semi-finals.

The 18-year-old midfielder’s confusion with “they” and “we” makes sense. Until last season, she was on the outside looking in.

Serrajordi was used to watching the first team from her home at La Masia, the famed Barcelona youth academy known for fostering top talent in the club’s pipeline. The team’s six previous Champions League final appearances were games she only saw from afar. Less than a decade ago, she waited anxiously in the stands to see if the team’s all-time leading goalscorer, Alexia Putellas, would take a photo with her.

But in the span of a year, her daydreams became a reality.

She has gone from taking photos with Putellas to forming the starting midfield trio alongside her and Patri Guijarro — both of whom she calls her big sisters. Together they won Liga F, Copa de la Reina, the Spanish Super Cup and the Champions League.

Next season, she will face yet another tall task, becoming her idol’s successor after Putellas’ recent move to England’s London City Lionesses. It requires filling the shoes of a player who spent 14 years at Barcelona, won 38 trophies and is the club’s joint-second highest all-time scorer, alongside Cesar Rodriguez on 232 goals, trailing only Lionel Messi (672).

Barcelona knew players like Putellas, Mapi Leon and Ona Batlle would leave and wanted to protect itself from yet another offseason of departures. However, without the financial resources to sign players without restraint, Barcelona are limited by an economic crisis affecting the entire club.

Instead, they looked to players coming through from La Masia. Players like Serrajordi.

Serrajordi passed her first test, replacing Bonmati, the current Ballon d’Or holder, after she suffered a transverse fracture of her left fibula in November and was out until May. The youngster stepped up for her club and made her debut for Spain’s senior national team aged 17.

In the second leg of the Champions League semi-finals, she controlled both legs against Bayern Munich from the centre of midfield. After that 5-3 aggregate victory, at the age of 18 and 167 days, she became the youngest Barcelona player to start a Champions League final against OL Lyonnes in Oslo.

She played 42 matches in her first season with the senior team, starting 25, scoring twice and providing two assists. Her achievements make her a clear contender to succeed team-mate Vicky Lopez as the next Golden Girl, the annual award given to the best female under-21 football player in Europe.

Some within the club describe Serrajordi as a cross between Putellas and Guijarro. They even joke that she looks like a combination of the two. Though she started as an attacking midfielder, in recent years in Barcelona’s youth teams, she has played as a defensive midfielder.

Described as having a powerful physique, keen ball-winning skills and an ability to anticipate the next play. She possesses technical ability and excellent dribbling skills to break through opposition lines.

The tactical astuteness is not limited to the pitch. As a child, she used to compete in chess tournaments, but eventually gave it up due to the commitment. She even played chess against Barcelona men’s player Pedri for a commercial. The two share an agency, Leaderbrock Sports Group. Her father, upon seeing the pictures of the game, asked, “Did you beat Pedri at least?”

Despite still being in her teens, Serrajordi rarely checks social media to see what people are saying about her. Instead, she maintains her calm, taking everything that’s happened over the last year in her stride.

Others might have been overwhelmed by such a daunting challenge, but Serrajordi approached it with the positive, carefree attitude of a younger generation, similar to Lamine Yamal, Lopez or Pau Cubarsi.

When Serrajordi returned to Spain after winning her first Champions League title, she went back to La Masia, where she will continue to live until November of this year. However, replacing a player she looked up to is not just difficult on the pitch, but challenging in other ways.

When Putellas announced her departure, Serrajordi was one of the most emotional players at the farewell ceremony, despite having played alongside her for just one season.

“I would have liked to have made it (into the first team) sooner,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara in May. “But I think she’s done everything she needed to do at Barça and has opened many doors for us.”

During the Champions League celebrations, before La Reina’s departure was public, Putellas was very active in supporting Serrajordi, including featuring her in a post-game, pre-party Instagram Live.

The day after returning from Oslo, Norway, Putellas used her time on the microphone during the public celebrations at Montjuïc to chant, “Serra, Serra.”

“We baked the cake and now they’ll eat it,” Putellas said, looking at her younger team-mates during her farewell.

“Becoming a new Alexia is impossible for me. There will never be anyone like her. That much is clear, and I don’t put that pressure on myself because I won’t be her,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara.

Still, with her presence, the future of Barcelona’s midfield remains in safe hands.

This article originally appeared in The Athletic.

2026 The Athletic Media Company

title: What Stephen A. Smith told the ESPN ‘bosses’ after learning of Ryan Clark’s firing
description: Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development. It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.
fullText:
Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development.

It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.

Clark has been a regular on ESPN having joined the network all the way back in 2015 following a successful career as a safety in the NFL.

However, the nature of Clark’s departure is what has really frustrated some of his colleagues as he was informed of the decision midway through an NFL Live show on ESPN.

It has been suggested that Clark’s confrontational manner contributed to his ESPN firing following a heated exchange with Peter Schrager last year.

However, established talking head Stephen A. Smith has now weighed in on the matter to share his frustrations with the ESPN chiefs.

Clark is just one of 10 members of the ESPN broadcasting team that has been laid off since the start of the week as the cost-cutting exercise takes effect.

Smith has established himself as one of the top voices on ESPN, which is why his latest comments on Clark’s firing will hold some value.

“A person I considered to be the best NFL analyst in America, is no longer a part of ESPN. I can’t even tell y’all how much that hurt,” said Smith on his YouTube channel.

“I’m gonna miss him. I’m not happy with this decision. The bosses know I’m not happy. I made this very clear.”

The departures from ESPN are expected to continue over the coming days as their cutbacks provide some shocking layoffs.

It has even been suggested that NFL Insider Tom Pelissero is one of the names who could follow Clark out of the doors at ESPN.

There are some seriously established figures in the American sports broadcasting business who are heading into the summer as free agents.

Someone like Pelissero would unlikely be out of work for too long, but it does go to show the extent to which ESPN are going to in order to cut back on their costs.

Roger Goodell’s $64m NFL salary to explode as masterplan for 18-game season accelerates

title: Dustin Poirier believes airport arrest could have been blessing in disguise
description: Former interim lightweight champion and fan favorite Dustin Poirier has struggled adjusting to life outside of fighting. "The Diamond" retired from competition following his UFC 318 loss to Max Hollway last July. His struggles were on full display on June 21. "The Diamond" was removed from a flight ...
fullText:
Former interim lightweight champion and fan favorite Dustin Poirier has struggled adjusting to life outside of fighting. "The Diamond" retired from competition following his UFC 318 loss to Max Hollway last July. His struggles were on full display on June 21.

"The Diamond" was removed from a flight for public intoxication at an Atlanta airport and arrested. Video of the incident went viral showing Poirier threatening police officers. A month after the arrest, Poirier say's he's 'good' and working on himself.

"Everything is good, man. I'm working on myself day in day out and it's a thing that's never fixed, you know? I've been dealing with mental stuff for years," Poirier said on The Ariel Helwani Show. "I'm doing everything I need to do. I have a great support system, and I'm good. Me and my family are good. Everything's good on my side."

While the incident was embarrassing and led to lost opportunities, Poirier believes maybe it needed to happen. It ended up being a wakeup call.

"Things happen. We live. We grow, and it's something that happened that's going to change my life. I know I've said this a few times on different shows, but it's something that maybe it needed to happen," Poirier said.

"Now my son, my daughter is never going to see me intoxicated like I saw my father. You know, it's just they're breaking a, breaking a family curse and I'm going to be the one to break it. It stops here."

"I don't believe it's something that's ever fixed. There's no eureka moment. There's no magic pill. There's no one day you wake up and the mental hurdles are gone. It's something you have to live with and work on every day. Every single day I have to read and write and do the things that I learned through therapy that made me feel better, that helped me control my thoughts. I just go away from it once I started feeling well mentally," he said.

title: Warriors Proposed to Land Trey Murphy in 3-Team Trade As LeBron Moves On
description: With LeBron James dream nearly dead, this Warriors trade pitch lands Trey Murphy III, the Bay Area franchise's coveted "dream" target. The post Warriors Proposed to Land Trey Murphy in 3-Team Trade As LeBron Moves On appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
Amid increasing chatter of LeBron James narrowing his free-agency options to the Miami Heat and Cleveland Cavaliers, the time has arrived for the Golden State Warriors to explore other avenues to improve their roster around Stephen Curry and Draymond Green.

Enter Trey Murphy III: a player who has hovered on the Warriors’ radar for years and remains a widely cited “dream target” who could now be up for grabs.

According to The Athletic’s John Hollinger, rival teams are expected to gauge the New Orleans Pelicans‘ willingness to trade the sharpshooting wing once the LeBron James free-agency sweepstakes conclude.

“Chatter about a Trey Murphy trade, while lessened, hasn’t vanished and is one of many potential pieces of league business that could resurface once LeBron James’ next destination becomes clear,” Hollinger reported on Monday.

Amid the reports, a Warriors trade idea doing the rounds would see the franchise part with Brandin Podziemski, Moses Moody and multiple first-round picks (the Pelicans are reportedly seeking up to three) in order to land Murphy.

Warriors would receive: Trey Murphy III

Pelicans would receive: Moses Moody (via NTMLE), Myles Turner, unrestricted 2027 first-round pick (via GSW), 2029 first-round pick (via GSW) and 2031 first-round pick (via GSW)

Bucks would receive: Brandin Podziemski

Some would argue that giving up three first-round picks, Podz and Moody just for Murphy is an astronomical asking price. Unfortunately for the Warriors, they’re in a desperate situation as Stephen Curry will turn 39 midway through the 2026-27 season, and Draymond Green and Jimmy Butler III aren’t getting any younger.

A player like Murray — who thrives as a movement shooter — will obviously be the ideal fit next to Curry, Butler, Green and Porzingis, forming a potent starting five.

It’s also now widely accepted that neither Podziemski nor Moody have established themselves as key pieces of Golden State’s future core, making them dispensable.

The Pelicans were forced to bring Zion Williamson off the bench last season due to their frontcourt logjam, which includes Herbert Jones, Derik Queen, Yves Messi and Saddiq Bey. At some point, Murphy will likely be better off playing elsewhere, especially if not being utilized to the best of his abilities in New Orleans.

Also, the Pels don’t hurt their future at all by grabbing three first-round picks from Golden State. The move would actually open up the possibility of the franchise trading other veterans like Williamson, Jordan Poole, Dejounte Murray and Jones, and giving the keys to their coveted young duo of Queen and Jeremiah Fears.

One of the few teams still flush with cap space, the Bucks have nothing to lose by onboarding Podziemski, who hits restricted free agency next year. If Podz doesn’t gel with their young core of Ryan Rollins, Brayden Burries, Nate Ament, Kel’el Ware, Kasparas Jakučionis and Jaime Jaquez Jr, they can simply let him walk next year.

The southpaw has definitely flashed enough potential in his three years in Golden State to get a look-in from the Bucks or any other young team.

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

This article was originally published on HEAVY

The post Warriors Proposed to Land Trey Murphy in 3-Team Trade As LeBron Moves On appeared first on HEAVY.

title: Fitness emergency! Nearly a dozen Indian stars called to CoE ahead of Sri Lanka tests
description: Nearly a dozen Indian cricketers, including Mohammed Siraj, Ravindra Jadeja, and Hardik Pandya, undergo mandatory fitness tests at the CoE ahead of the Sri Lanka Test tour.
fullText:
Fitness emergency! Nearly a dozen Indian stars called to CoE ahead of Sri Lanka tests originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Nearly a dozen Indian cricketers set to assemble at CoE this week.

Fitness checks planned before Test tour of Sri Lanka next month.

BCCI wants no further breakdowns ahead of packed 2027 calendar.

There has been a reported uptick in activity at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru, with the facility gearing up to welcome a large group of contracted and shortlisted players over the coming days.

According to The Times of India, a dozen Indian cricketers are expected to be present at the facility by the end of this week, each there for slightly different reasons.

Several names are already stationed at the centre, including Hardik Pandya, Nitish Kumar Reddy, Varun Chakaravarthy, Harshit Rana, and Akash Deep. They are set to be joined shortly by Washington Sundar and a handful of others as preparations intensify before a gruelling calendar.

The BCCI wants to complete a round of fitness assessments for players in contention for next month's Test tour of Sri Lanka.

Mohammed Siraj is due to arrive at the facility this week, with Ravindra Jadeja potentially following soon after. Meanwhile, several targeted players, including Sarfaraz Khan, Musheer Khan, and Abhishek Porel, have already completed their evaluations, and staff at the centre anticipate even more arrivals over the coming days, particularly before these cricketers head off to represent their respective zonal sides in the Duleep Trophy.

MORE: No update in fitness SOP since 2023: BCCI CoE under the scanner after fitness issues in India camp

The BCCI is determined not to leave anything to chance regarding player fitness ahead of the Sri Lanka Test series, with comprehensive assessments planned before selectors finalise their squad for the two-match series.

"Hardik has been there training, Reddy is recovering, and Varun and Harshit are undergoing their rehabilitation," a source told ToI.

"The team management doesn't want to take any chances with regard to its players ahead of the Sri Lanka Test series.
Thorough assessments will be carried out, and the selectors will be informed about their status before the team is picked for the two Tests. The CoE has a fair idea of the targeted players after the red-ball tournament in Bengaluru."

India continue to grapple with fitness concerns as they build toward the 2027 World Cup. Naturally, the last thing the setup wants is further injury breakdowns either before or during the crucial upcoming Test series.

Most centrally contracted players are staring down an extremely packed schedule ahead, spanning all three formats, including an extended tour of New Zealand comprising five T20Is, five ODIs, and two Tests.

Taking this into consideration, the management is keen on maintaining a wide, fully assessed pool of players ready to be called upon, since multiple squads will likely need to be fielded simultaneously across different assignments in the months ahead.

MORE: Tilak Varma reveals how playing mostly in Asia exposed India's batting struggles in T20I series

This proactive round of fitness testing feels like a long-overdue correction rather than routine practice, especially after a year littered with hamstring strains, quad injuries, and rushed comebacks.

Gathering nearly a dozen players together for thorough assessment before a Test series, rather than scrambling after an injury strikes mid-tour, signals the board finally treating fitness as a planning priority instead of an afterthought. What will matter most going forward, though, is whether this becomes a consistent practice tied to every major assignment, rather than a one-off response triggered by recent embarrassment in England.

With a brutal, multi-format calendar stretching well into the World Cup year, India simply cannot afford another wave of last-minute withdrawals. This week's activity at the CoE looks like a genuine step toward accountability, but only sustained follow-through will prove whether real lessons have actually been learned.

For all the latest cricket news, opinion, and commentary and to share your voice, head to our Facebook, Instagram, and X (Twitter) pages.

title: Posts falsely claim Lamine Yamal snubbed Donald Trump after World Cup win
description: Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.
fullText:
Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.

"Lamine Yamal did not shake hands with Trump; he avoided the US president," says the Bengali-language caption of a YouTube video shared on July 20, 2026.

The video, which has been viewed more than 334,000 times, appears to show Spain's 19-year-old winger Lamine Yamal going to collect his World Cup winner's medal. He is first met by FIFA head Gianni Infantino before briefly stopping in front of US President Donald Trump, who drapes the medal over Yamal's head.

According to the video's Bengali-language voiceover, Yamal did not shake Trump's hand. It speculates this may be because of Trump's "foreign policy towards Palestinians".

The clip was also shared in similar YouTube and Facebook posts after Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina in the World Cup final on July 19, with the US president then presenting the World Cup trophy to La Roja ( archived link ).

"Lamine Yamal did the right thing 100 percent," says a comment on one of the posts, while another says "Thanks Spain, and thanks Yamal."

There is strong support for the Palestinian people in Muslim-majority Bangladesh, and the government reaffirmed its "steadfast commitment to the legitimate rights of the Palestinian people and the establishment of an independent and sovereign State of Palestine" in July ( archived link ).

Yamal has also expressed support for the Palestinian cause in the past, most recently during his club side Barcelona's title celebrations in May when he waved a Palestinian flag during an open-top bus parade ( archived link ).

Trump, meanwhile, has firmly backed Israel despite growing international outrage over its offensive in the Palestinian territory of Gaza, in response to the October 7, 2023 attacks carried out by Gaza-based militants Hamas ( archived link ).

There was an international  backlash when Trump said he wanted to take control of the Gaza strip and "permanently" resettle Palestinians in other countries ( archived link ).

The US president's Gaza peace plan -- which was endorsed by the UN Security Council in November 2025 -- does, however, include a path to a future Palestinian state ( archived link ).

The claims circulating on social media that Yamal avoided shaking Trump's hand are false, with the angle the falsely shared video appearing to have obscured the handshake.

Several  AFP photos , taken from a different angle to the falsely shared video, show  Yamal and Trump shaking hands as the teenager received his winner's medal.

Moreover, a  keyword search on Google led to similar footage shared on the verified YouTube channels of the White House and Fox Sports showing the pair shaking hands (archived  here and here ).

title: 'Rogers departs on a high... but reinvestment critical for next chapter'
description: Aston Villa are no strangers to selling some of their most prized assets.  It wasn't too long ago that the club parted ways with academy darling Jack Grealish for a then British record £100m.  More recently, there were PSR-juggling deals for Douglas Luiz and Jacob Ramsey.
fullText:
Aston Villa are no strangers to selling some of their most prized assets.

It wasn't too long ago that the club parted ways with academy darling Jack Grealish for a then British record £100m. More recently, there were PSR-juggling deals for Douglas Luiz and Jacob Ramsey.

While Morgan Rogers' departure is another bitter reminder of the financial gap between Villa and their peers, it is a mammoth profit on a young player at the opportune moment.

Rogers arrived at Villa as a relative unknown - a Manchester City academy graduate with a year in the Championship under his belt. He was thrown in at the deep end and quickly became a guaranteed starter under Unai Emery.

In his two and a half years in claret and blue, Rogers amassed well in excess of 100 appearances, more than 50 goal contributions, a PFA Young Player of the Year award and, most importantly, a European trophy.

There is no doubt Emery is losing one of the most exciting young British talents of the past few years. The Midlands boy has had a significant impact on the team, and for that he will be etched into club history.

However, for too long, those in charge at Villa have failed to capitalise on player values at the right time. The £117m fee, with a portion heading back to Middlesborough, is a sum that helps towards a partial squad rebuild, with Johan Manzambi and Joao Gomes already through the doors.

This comes at a pivotal moment, too, as Emery looks to build on last season's European glory. The squad that finished the last campaign was aging, with many players on high wages and running deep into their contracts. A refresh of faces felt inevitable.

Football is a business, now more than ever, and there is very little room for sentimentality.

For fans, Rogers departs on a high, and leaves behind a plethora of unforgettable memories. From here, recruitment and reinvestment of receipts will be critical for the next chapter of success at Aston Villa.

Find more from Hannah Gowen at UTV

title: The carpenter aiming for Commonwealth boxing glory
description: BBC Sport meets Tommy Teers, who is balancing his job as a carpenter with training to box at the 2026 Commonwealth Games for Guernsey.
fullText:
But by night the 28-year-old trains in the gym with the aim of bringing boxing glory to Guernsey at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow.

"I started boxing just to keep fit and to get to this stage is crazy," Teers tells BBC Guernsey as he take a break from training in the gym.

"I've put the hard work in and everyone around me has supported me, so I feel like it's time."

But the hard work for Teers is not just confined to the gym, it is what he does during the day as he balances his sport and carpentry.

Guernsey select team for 2026 Commonwealth Games

He boxes three nights a week, does strength and conditioning every night in-between before either doing sprints or a long run at the weekend.

"It's hard balancing everything, but I'm doing what I can," he says.

"The guys I'll be fighting, they're full-time athletes, but I can't afford to do that.

"I've got my business to run and that's kind of what I want to do, so doing this will be a good experience for me to see what level I can actually get to."

Teers and Niall Adams will fight for Guernsey as part of their 20-strong team going to Glasgow.

The bantamweight has twice reached the English Elite National semi-finals and has fought Ellis Trowbridge, who has been selected to represent England in Glasgow.

"I'm just going to give it my all, perform as best as I can that's all I can ask for really," he says.

"I know the competition's going to be hard, it's definitely a step up for me.

"But that's what I want, I want to see how good I can get and how I can compete against those guys."

title: 'Chelsea are the biggest club in London' - Rogers
description: New Chelsea signing Morgan Rogers says he is "really excited" about "where the club is heading" under new manager Xabi Alonso.  The Blues completed the signing of England midfielder Rogers from Aston Villa for a British record transfer fee of £117m on Monday.  It had been widely expected that Rogers, who was Villa's most prized asset, would be available only for an English transfer record fee, but many expected Arsenal to be the frontrunners.
fullText:
New Chelsea signing Morgan Rogers says he is "really excited" about "where the club is heading" under new manager Xabi Alonso.

The Blues completed the signing of England midfielder Rogers from Aston Villa for a British record transfer fee of £117m on Monday.

It had been widely expected that Rogers, who was Villa's most prized asset, would be available only for an English transfer record fee, but many expected Arsenal to be the frontrunners.

However, Chelsea had been tracking Rogers for more than two years and had quietly made him their top attacking target with a combined effort involving the club's sporting directors, Alonso and co-owner Behdad Eghbali helping to secure a deal.

"I'm so excited," Rogers said on the move.

"For me, Chelsea are the biggest club in London and a club I've always admired since I was a kid.

"I'm really excited about the project with the new manager, the players we've got and where the club is heading. That's why I'm here and I can't wait to get started.

"The way the club wants to go and how it wants to move forward, it's something I want to be a part of and I think it was just perfect for me.

"I'm a free spirit, free-flowing footballer. I like to take risks, make things happen, change games. Whatever that may take. Just can't wait to get started."

title: 'Thank you for all the unforgettable memories' - Anderson says goodbye
description: Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.  Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup.  BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.
fullText:
Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.

Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup. BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.

The 23-year-old made 92 appearances across two seasons at Forest after joining from boyhood club Newcastle in 2024, helping them qualify for Europe in 2024-25 and reach the Europa League semi-finals the following year.

The goodbye message posted by Anderson on social media read: "Forest family,

"Firstly I am so grateful for the love and support you have shown me over the last 2 years especially towards the end of this season. From the minute I walked through the door I fell in love with this club.

"I want to thank Mr Marinakas, the staff and all players for welcoming me and making my time here so enjoyable, this club gave me the opportunity to grow as a player and a person.

"Bringing the club European football again after 29 years is what all of you fans deserved and to be a helping hand in that is so special.

"Nottingham forest will always have a special place in my heart. Thank you for all the unforgettable memories."

title: The most expensive football transfers of all time
description: Chelsea have finalised a deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa for a fee of £117m. The transfer will be a guaranteed sum and make Rogers the most expensive English footballer of all time, along...
fullText:
Chelsea have finalised a deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa for a fee of £117m. The transfer will be a guaranteed sum and make Rogers the most expensive English footballer of all time, alongside one of the most expensive in football history.

The deal followsElliot Anderson's £116m transfer from Nottingham Forest to Manchester City earlier this summer, as transfer fees continue to reach new heights.

Both deals still fall somewhat short of the world record.Paris Saint-Germain obliterated the world transfer record to sign Neymar from Barcelona in 2017 for a staggering £200m. Almost a decade on, the Brazilian's big-money move remains the benchmark.

All figures are guaranteed transfer fees exclusive of potential add-ons

title: “Chelsea are the biggest club in London”: Morgan Rogers seals £117m move and vows “I can’t wait to get started”
description: Morgan Rogers has said Chelsea are the biggest club in London after signing for the Blues in a £117m club record deal.Rogers has completed his transfer to Chelsea from Aston Villa in a deal that make...
fullText:
Morgan Rogers has said Chelsea are the biggest club in London after signing for the Blues in a £117m club record deal.

Rogers has completed his transfer to Chelsea from Aston Villa in a deal that makes the 23-year-old the most expensive English footballer of all time.

The England international arrives at Stamford Bridge after helping England to third place at the 2026 World Cup this summer.

He has established himself as one of the most promising talents in thePremier League in recent seasons, earning the PFA Young Player of the Year in 2024/25.

Last season, he was named the Europa League Player of the Season after helping Aston Villa end a 30-year trophy drought with success in Europe.

He scored 10 goals and created six 

"I'm so excited," Rogers toldchelseafc.com.

"For me, Chelsea are the biggest club in London and a club I've always admired since I was a kid.

"I'm really excited about the project with the new manager, the players we've got and where the club is heading. That's why I'm here and I can't wait to get started."

title: Real Madrid defender’s physical trainer assures he wants to stay despite exit rumours: ‘He wants to play a key role and succeed’
description: Raul Asencio has no intention of walking away from Real Madrid despite growing uncertainty surrounding his future at the Santiago Bernabeu. The young defender continues to be linked with a summer exi...
fullText:
Raul Asencio has no intention of walking away from Real Madrid despite growing uncertainty surrounding his future at the Santiago Bernabeu.

The young defender continues to be linked with a summer exit after reportedly being informed by Jose Mourinho that he is not part of the manager’s plans for the upcoming season.

While speculation over his future has intensified in recent weeks, those closest to the 23-year-old insist his mindset has never changed.

Rather than looking for a move elsewhere, Asencio remains focused on earning his place at Real Madrid and proving he deserves an opportunity under Mourinho.

Speaking in an interview with AS, the defender’s physical trainer, Luiso Hernandez, has now shed light on Asencio’s mentality ahead of a crucial pre-season.

Asencio has been at the centre of transfer speculation throughout the summer following reports that Mourinho does not see him as part of his plans for next season.

Asencio is linked with a summer exit from Real Madrid. (Photo by Jose Manuel Alvarez Rey/Getty Images)

Despite that setback, Hernandez revealed the defender has continued preparing with complete focus, refusing to allow rumours surrounding his future to affect his mentality.

“The key has been to treat the situation as normal.

“To understand that Real Madrid is the most demanding environment in the world and that, when you’re at Madrid, you’re exposed to the possibility of this sort of thing happening.”

The trainer explained that uncertainty is part of life at one of the biggest clubs in world football, making mental resilience just as important as physical preparation.

“The reality is that, despite all the external noise that might be generated, you’re here today and you might not be here tomorrow,” he began saying.

Hernandez also made it clear that Asencio has never considered giving up on his dream of making it at Real Madrid.

The defender wishes to succeed at Santiago Bernabeu. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

“He has always made it clear to me that he wants to stay at Madrid, play a key role and succeed.

“That’s why we’ve worked to ensure he’s in the best possible shape for this pre-season, which is a new and different situation for all the players at Madrid.”

The trainer believes the arrival of a new coaching staff offers every player an opportunity to impress, regardless of what happened during the previous campaign.

“Although last season wasn’t the best, the picture is now completely different because everyone is starting on an equal footing.

“Beyond that, I believe Raul’s profile fits perfectly with the new coaching staff’s vision,” he concluded.

title: Man in court accused of racist post about Lioness
description: The 19-year-old is accused of using racist language in a post about the footballer.
fullText:
A teenager has appeared in court charged with making a racist comment on Instagram about England defender Jess Carter.

Aaryan Agarwal allegedly sent the footballer a message in July last year, during Women's Euro 2025.

The 19-year-old of Dickens Rise, Chigwell, is charged with a single count of sending a letter, communication, or article conveying an indecent or offensive message.

He appeared at Colchester Magistrates' Court on Tuesday but did not enter a plea. He is due to attend Chelmsford Crown Court on 18 August.

Magistrates were told the message was sent on 17 July, when England won against Sweden in their quarter-final.

The post allegedly referred to Carter using racist language.

Agarwal was arrested in September after the message was reported.

Carter, who is from Warwick, was part of the England squad who created history by winning their first major women's tournament at Euro 2022.

The 28-year-old spent six years at Chelsea before moving to US-based side Gotham FC in 2024.

Correction 22 July: This article was corrected to clarify that the charge relates to one message, rather than the previously reported two.
